text:
Olympicshowjumper Henrik von Eckermann was slammed for his ‘unbelievably bad riding’ after ‘spooking’ horse King Edward and being dismounted in theParisfinal.

Sweden’s Von Eckermann was expected to challenge for the medals in the individual jumping final, which took place on Tuesday in the grounds of the Palace of Versaille.

Von Eckermann is ranked No. 1 in the world and looked to have an excellent chance of medalling on talented horse King Edward.

They safely and impressively made their way over seven fences in the final before the run started to go badly wrong, the crowd gasping as King Edward hesitated over jump eight.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

Von Eckermann managed to get King Edward over the next fence but all rhythm had been lost and the horse dramatically pulled up, sending his rider flying and almost crashing into a fence.

‘He’s changed his mind, he went to go forward,’ BBC commentator Andy Austin said as the run began to go wrong. ‘This is unbelievably strange and bad riding from Henrik von Eckermann.

‘What is he doing?! He’s fallen off. I have never seen anything like this in my life. This is the world No.1, he completely messed up his ride.

Just wanted to show this video of Henrik von Eckermann, the world no.1. Not into horse riding so I have no clue what happened here if anyone can tell me.#Olympics2024Paris#Olympics#Equestrianpic.twitter.com/OomqSBMLbW

‘What is going on? He changed his mind and it completely unsettled and spooked King Edward. He was out of control when he jumped the number eight.

‘I have never seen drama like it. Extraordinary. Henrik thankfully looks okay, so does the horse.

‘He makes a terrible mistake, rides quite crazily out of control to the fence and then falls off because his horse spooks.

‘You couldn’t make it up. If you wanted to tune in for some drama, you’re certainly getting it. That was quite unbelievable.’

Von Eckermann looked shocked and a little dazed following the fall but he and King Edward were thankfully unharmed and left the arena together.

Just before Von Eckermann took to the course he was described as the ‘most bankable rider’ in the showjumping final.

‘One thing is for sure, this guy is not going to be fazed, even at this stage of the competition,’ another commentator said before the dramatic fall.

‘He’s not going to be fazed. He’s got nerves of steel and a horse with talent, over to you, Henrik von Eckermann.

‘We said we wasn’t going to do big build-ups but we just had to with this pair.’

Paris 2024 is Von Eckermann’s third Olympic Games. At Tokyo the two-time world champion won gold in the team event and finished fourth in the individual competition.

title: BBC commentator slams 'unbelievably bad riding' that 'spooked' horse at Olympics | Metro News
description: Olympic showjumper Henrik von Eckermann was slammed for his 'unbelievably bad riding' after 'spooking' his horse in the Paris final.
